description_long: This jersey barrier is a robust, rectangular structure with a sloped base and a flat top, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The front and rear faces are painted with bold diagonal red and white stripes, while the sides and top feature a weathered blue and gray finish, showing signs of wear and scuffing. Two metal lifting hooks are visible on the top edge, indicating it can be moved with machinery. The surface is rough and matte, with visible scratches and paint chipping, suggesting frequent outdoor use. The barrier is constructed primarily from concrete, with possible internal reinforcement and hollow sections for reduced weight or filling with water or sand. Its size and mass make it a formidable obstacle for vehicles and pedestrians, while its flat top can support small objects.
description: A large, weathered concrete jersey barrier with red and white diagonal stripes on one side and blue-gray paint on the other, featuring metal lifting hooks and visible surface wear.
description_view_0: The barrier's blue-gray painted side is visible, along with two metal hooks on top and minor scuff marks; a yellow 'No Parking' sign and orange traffic cone are nearby.
description_view_1: Shows the blue-gray rear face with more pronounced weathering and both metal hooks, with the red and white striped face partially visible on the right edge.
description_view_2: Displays the bold red and white diagonal striped face, with the yellow 'No Parking' sign and orange cone to the right.
description_view_3: Highlights the red and white striped face, with the yellow 'No Parking' sign and orange cone in the foreground.
